puffin: Puffins are small with distinctive brightly colored orange beaks, often standing on rocks outside of the water or swimming on the surface of the water. They also have long waterproof feathers that help them swim and fly.
shark: [{'shark': 'A large, elongated fish with a streamlined body, a pointed snout, a dorsal fin, a smooth, shiny skin, and a powerful tail that propels it through the water. Sharks are typically found in marine environments, swimming in schools or alone, and are not characterized by a rounded body, flippers, or a short, thick neck. The object in the blue bounding box is a great white shark, while the object in the green bounding box is a hammerhead shark, both fitting the general description of a shark with their streamlined bodies and powerful tails. The updated class definition should make it easier to accurately identify all true instances of the "shark" class similar to the one seen in the blue and green bounding boxes, while excluding stingrays like the one in the red bounding box.'}]
starfish: [{'starfish': 'A starfish is characterized by its five-pointed shape, central body, and slender arms extending outward. Starfish are typically found in marine environments, often attached to rocks or other surfaces. They have a smooth, fleshy texture and can come in various colors, including shades of blue, orange, and brown. The distinctive feature that differentiates starfish from other objects in the scene is their unique five-pointed shape and the way they attach to surfaces. The starfish in the images have a more elongated and slender arm structure compared to the starfish in the red bounding box, which has a more robust and rounded arm structure.'}]
stingray: [{'stingray': 'A stingray is a flat, disc-shaped fish with a long, slender tail and a pair of pectoral fins that resemble wings. It is typically found in shallow waters near the ocean floor, where it feeds on small fish, crustaceans, and mollusks.'}]
